_comment: Generated by the Japan Trip itinerary editor. Edit via the webpage or directly in this file.

days.label: Day 1: 22 Apr (Wed)
days.title: Arrival Day — Welcome to Tokyo
days.desc: A travel day — the adventure begins! Fly in from Singapore and ease into Tokyo with a light first evening near your hotel.
days.tip: Clear immigration and collect luggage by ~18:30–19:00. Don't plan anything demanding — a short walk and a good dinner is the perfect first night.

stops.name: Depart Singapore Changi (SIN)
stops.note:
Singapore Airlines SQ12
Departs at 09:15, arrives at Narita Terminal 1 at 17:30
Flight departs from Changi Airport T3
3 x Business, 2 x Economy

stops.name: Arrive Tokyo Narita (NRT)
stops.note:
Arrive at Narita International Airport Terminal 1.
Expect 45-60min for immigration and baggage claim.

stops.name: Board Airport Limousine bus to Hotel
stops.note:
Purchase Limousine Bus tickets from ticket counters on 1F Arrival Lobby (both South & North Wings).
Departing bus timings: 18:25, 18:55, 19:25
Limousine bus takes around 1.5hrs to 2hours, and brings us directly to our hotel.
More info on bus timings

stops.name: Shinjuku Washington Hotel
stops.note: Check in to hotel & freshen up

stops.name: Late dinner near the hotel
stops.note: Walking distance from the hotel.

options.name: Tsukemen Gonokami Seisakusho
options.note:
Understated, compact restaurant offering bowls of ramen noodles in a relaxed ambiance. Slight further from hotel (1.3km walk, 19min)
Closes at 9pm

options.name: Fūunji Shinjuku
options.note:
Tsukemen dipping noodles with a choice of sauces, served at the counter of this busy ramen shop. 6min walk away.
Closes at 9pm

options.name: Beef Kitchen Stand Nishi-Shinjuku
options.note:
Mostly beef, but has other options too. Right under the hotel.
Closes at 11pm.

options.name: Shinpachi Shokudo Shinjuku
options.note:
Syokudo and Teishoku restaurant. Able to order via the iPad, no conversation required. Grilled fish is good. 8min walk from hotel
Closes at 10.40pm

days.label: Day 2: 23 Apr (Thu)
days.title: Tokyo City
days.desc: Your first full day — get your bearings with green spaces and a gentle stroll through one of Tokyo's most atmospheric old neighbourhoods.

stops.name: Meiji Jingu
stops.note:
Free, serene forest walk. Arrive earlier to beat the crowds.
The forested shrine approach is serene and uncrowded before 9am. Budget about 1 hour. Free entry.

stops.name: Takeshita Street, Harajuku
stops.note: The famous pedestrian street for quirky fashion, crepes, and people-watching. Just 10 min walk from Meiji Jingu. Fun even if you don't buy anything — best experienced before the afternoon crowds arrive.

stops.name: Google Japan - Shibuya Office
stops.note: 10 min walk south from Harajuku

stops.name: Sumida Park / Chidorigafuchi
stops.note:
20 min from Shibuya. Moat walk along the Imperial Palace, rowboats available (~¥800/30 min). About 1 hour.

Sumida Park stretches along both sides of the Sumida River and is particularly stunning in late April. Provides one of the best unobstructed photo spots of the Tokyo Skytree across the water.

stops.name: Hamarikyu Gardens
stops.note: 20 min east from Chidorigafuchi. Beautiful traditional garden on Tokyo Bay. ¥300 entry. Board the water bus here.

stops.name: Water Bus: Hamarikyu → Asakusa
stops.note: 40min cruise up the Sumida River past 12 bridges with Skytree appearing on the horizon as you arrive.

stops.name: Sumida Park stroll
stops.note: Right off the water bus pier. Skytree views, the Asahi golden flame building, riverside walk.

stops.name: Asakusa Senso-ji
stops.note:
Senso-ji is Tokyo’s oldest and most significant Buddhist temple, dedicated to Kannon, the Bodhisattva of Mercy. Entering through the massive Kaminarimon (Thunder Gate) is a quintessential Tokyo experience. The approach to the temple, known as Nakamise-dori, is a historic shopping street lined with dozens of stalls selling traditional snacks like age-manju (fried bean cakes) and authentic souvenirs.
Kaminarimon gate and Nakamise street as the lanterns come on. The most atmospheric time to visit.

stops.name: Buy breakfast from convenience store
stops.note: Early start the next day, prepare some sandwiches or snacks.

days.label: Day 3: 24 Apr (Fri)
days.title: Day Trip: Izu
days.desc: More info: 1-Day Mt Omuro, Izu Tour

stops.name: Tour pick up
stops.note:
Tour pickup from hotel between 6.30am - 7.30am. Bus journey takes around 2hrs.
Breakfast starts at 6.30am, so we might need to prepare breakfast from the convenience store.

Klook Operator Whatsapp: 86-13657671089
Tour Guide: Chinese

stops.name: Mount Omuro & Cactus Park
stops.note:
Mount Omuro Crater Hiking Spectacle: Take a cable car (may be temporarily suspended due to strong winds or heavy rain) to the verdant summit and walk around the giant bowl-shaped crater for a 360-degree panoramic view of the magnificent Izu Peninsula and Sagami Bay. Its unique conical shape is considered one of the prototypes for “Lake Itomori” in “Your Name.”, adding a touch of animated romance to the journey.
Cactus Park: Adorable Pet Interaction Paradise: This is more than just cacti; it’s a paradise for close encounters with popular animals such as cute capybaras, lively meerkats, and leisurely red pandas.

stops.name: Izu Ocean View Train Experience
stops.note: Ride the distinctive “Resort 21” series sightseeing trains, such as the representative trains (Black Ship Train/Kinmedai Train), which feature extra-large observation windows, with some seats even rotating to face the ocean. Soaring above the Pacific: The train speeds along the Izu East Coast, with the boundless blue Pacific Ocean outside the window, like a dream journey into a scene from “Spirited Away.”

stops.name: Jogasaki Coast · Kadowaki Suspension Bridge
stops.note:
A Masterpiece of Volcanic Lava: The coast was formed by lava flows from Mount Omuro thousands of years ago, featuring steep cliffs, rugged reefs, and surging waves, full of natural, raw power.
Heart-Pounding Suspension Bridge Walk: Walk across the 48-meter-high Kadowaki Suspension Bridge, with the magnificent sight of waves crashing below and the whistling sea breeze in your ears. The dual shock of sight and sound makes it an excellent photo spot.

stops.name: Dinner
stops.note: Expected return time: around 6pm, directly to hotel

stops.name: Tsukiji Outer Market
stops.note: Arrive by 7–8am for peak freshness and full stall selection. Graze through fresh oysters, tamagoyaki, tuna sashimi, seafood skewers, strawberry daifuku. Most vendors wind down by noon so the early start is worth it.

stops.name: Kiri Yanaka
stops.note: 30 min by train north to Yanaka. A traditional Japanese house hidden at the end of an alley behind a wooden gate, opening onto a quiet garden. Unagi (eel) set meals, tatami seating, very serene atmosphere. One of the most atmospheric lunch spots in this part of Tokyo. Opens 11:30am. Book ahead — it fills up.

options.name: TAYORI
options.note: If Kiri Yanaka is unavailable, TAYORI on Yanaka Ginza is a great fallback — a charming deli-restaurant with weekly set meals for ¥1,550, hidden off a side alley. ⚠️ Closed Mon & Tue.

stops.name: Nezu Shrine
stops.note: 10 min walk. Start the afternoon here while it's quietest. The long tunnel of vermilion torii gates winds up a mossy hillside — intimate and unhurried compared to Fushimi Inari. Small pond garden and waterfall at the end of the path. Free, closes 5pm.

stops.name: SCAI The Bathhouse
stops.note: 10 min walk. A 200-year-old public bathhouse converted into a world-class contemporary art gallery. Free entry, Tue–Sat 12–6pm. ⚠️ Check their website before the day to confirm an exhibition is on.

stops.name: Yanaka Ginza
stops.note: 5 min walk north. Walk down the "Yuyake Dandan" sunset steps into the retro shopping street. Pick up menchi katsu from one of the stalls — it's the neighbourhood's signature snack. Browse local crafts, pottery, cat-themed everything. Mostly cash only.

stops.name: Yanakana Macha Stand
stops.note: Right on the street. Sit down for matcha latte, gelato or a crepe. Good rest stop before the train west.

stops.name: Shinjuku Gyoen National Garden
stops.note:
By late April, the Yaezakura (double-flowered chery blossoms), which are late-blooming cherry blossoms, reach their peak.
25 min from Tsukiji by train. One of Tokyo's best parks — a huge formal garden with Japanese, French and English sections. ¥500 entry. Perfect for a relaxed late morning walk after the Tsukiji bustle. Budget 1.5–2 hours. ⚠️ Closed Mondays.
Exit from the Shinjuku Gate side to walk toward Zauo.

options.name: Gyoen Roan
options.note: A robatayaki (charcoal grill) restaurant right by Shinjuku Gyoenmae Station. Specialises in grilled fish and vegetables cooked over charcoal in front of you — the chef at the open kitchen is part of the experience. Try the grilled fish collar, seasonal vegetables, and their sake selection. Cosy counter seating. Opens 5:30pm for dinner. Book ahead. ⚠️ Closed Sundays.

options.name: Tempura Satoumisatoyama
options.note: A short walk toward the Washington Hotel. Counter seating watching chefs fry tempura right in front of you. Excellent set menus covering shrimp, seasonal vegetables, fish — all cooked, nothing raw. Reviewers frequently go back multiple times in one trip. Opens 6pm for dinner. ⚠️ Closed Sundays.

options.name: Gyoen Imai — Kappo cuisine
options.note: A kappo (counter dining) restaurant near Shinjuku Gyoenmae. Set course menu at ¥9,500 covering grilled conger eel, shrimp dumplings, clay pot rice, and seasonal vegetables — all cooked. Elegant but not stuffy, and the clay pot rice at the end is a highlight. Opens 5pm. ⚠️ Closed Sundays.

stops.name: Pack bags — highway bus to Kawaguchiko tomorrow
stops.note: If possible, take only what you need. Leave the rest of the bags at the hotel.

days.label: Day 5: 26 Apr (Sun)
days.title: Arrival & First Fuji Views
days.desc: Travel to the Fuji Five Lakes region and spend a relaxed afternoon getting oriented with the best effortless view of Mt. Fuji. Driving Route
days.tip: With a rental car you are completely independent. Parking at Kawaguchiko attractions is generally free. Mt. Fuji mornings tend to be clearest — plan viewpoint stops before 11am.

stops.name: Highway bus: Shinjuku → Kawaguchiko
stops.note:
Depart hotel by 9.30am - 10-15min walk to Busta Shinjuku.
Bus departs 10.45am from Busta Shinjuku. Fully reserved seats. Journey takes around 1h45min

stops.name: Collect rental car
stops.note:
Toyota Rent-a-car Kawaguchiko Branch - 500m (7 min walk) away from Kawaguchiko Station. Mum & Koko to wait at the station with the luggages.
Sienta class vehicle with full insurance.

stops.name: Try hōtō noodles
stops.note: Several restaurants along the north shore have Fuji-view windows. Hōtō is Yamanashi's regional specialty: thick flat noodles in miso soup with pumpkin. A must-try.

options.name: Kosaku Kawaguchiko
options.note: (★4.2, 6,600+ reviews — most popular locally) — The one most locals and repeat visitors recommend. Served bubbling in a huge iron pot, miso-based broth with thick chewy noodles and big chunks of sweet pumpkin. You can get pork, duck, or chicken houtou here — not just the vegetarian version. Also does horse sashimi if you're adventurous. Traditional floor seating. Can sell out by evening so go at lunch. 10 min drive from the station.

options.name: Hotokura Funari Kawaguchiko
options.note: (★4.2, 3,300+ reviews) — The pick if you want the most atmospheric interior and the latest kitchen hours (open until 8:30pm). Chicken houtou is a standout, and they occasionally run specials like king crab houtou. Good side dishes for sharing. Queues can hit 45–60 min at dinner — arrive before 5:30pm or after 7:30pm.

options.name: Hotou Fudo
options.note: (★3.8) — The most famous chain in the area, directly opposite Kawaguchiko Station, so it's the most convenient. The dramatic thatched-roof building is iconic. But the rating tells the story — it's more tourist trap than destination, the base houtou is vegetarian-only (meat costs extra), and it's almost always queued. Fine if you just want the experience without going out of your way, but Kosaku is worth the short drive.

stops.name: Shimoyoshida Honcho Street
stops.note: This long retro Showa-era shopping street has a straight-line view of Fuji at the far end — one of those only-in-Japan compositions. Many shops are local and quirky. A reviewer notes it feels dead after 4pm, so arriving by 3:30 is ideal. Don't step into the road for photos — there's a traffic controller there for a reason, and you can get just as good a shot from the sidewalk where the road bends.

stops.name: Arakuyama Sengen Park (Chureito Pagoda)
stops.note:
398 steps up to the iconic five-storey pagoda with Mt. Fuji behind it. Best views before 10am. Steep climb. Free entry, parking ~¥1,000–1,500 if you park at the base of the shrine.
Online sources note that you will need to queue for a photo.

stops.name: Arakura Fuji Sengen Jinja Shrine
stops.note: Within Arakuyama Sengen Park

stops.name: Sunset at Oishi Park
stops.note: The late-April sunset behind Mt. Fuji from the north shore is exceptional. 10-minute drive from most accommodation. The north shore of the lake has the classic Fuji reflection view. Free entry and parking. Stay as long as the light holds.

options.name: Dino DINER
options.note: On the way home, this is a genuinely surprising find: a vintage American diner tucked into the Japanese countryside, complete with country music, floor cushions, and American memorabilia on the walls. The burgers are some of the best in the area, the onion rings and fried squid are must-orders, and the vibe is unlike anything else nearby. ⚠️ Closed Monday & Thursday. Opens for dinner at 6pm.

days.label: Day 6: 27 Apr (Mon)
days.title: Explore Mt Fuji 5 Lakes Area
days.desc: The best Fuji viewpoint in Japan and a timeless village of crystal-clear springs — a full varied day with something for everyone. Driving Route

stops.name: Kodachi Cafe
stops.note:
Breakfast cafe near the accoms. Opens at 7.30am. 
Cosy log cabin with Fuji views out the window. Try the cream mountain toast or fluffy french toast. Sells out fast! Closed Tue & Wed.

stops.name: Mt Fuji Panoramic Ropeway (Kachi-Kachi Yama)
stops.note: Buy tickets online in advance to skip the queue. Glides up to Tenjoyama Park for panoramic views of Kawaguchiko and Fuji. Shrine, love bell, and a swing at the top with a famous edge-of-the-sky photo spot. Opens 9am (sometimes 8:30am). About 1 hour including the ride up and down.

stops.name: Oishi Park & Nagasaki Park
stops.note:
Park at Oishi and walk 5 min west towards Nagasaki Park. Quieter and more photogenic than Oishi, with open unobstructed lake views. No dedicated parking so bikes or the short walk is the move.
Walk the Flower Road promenade.

stops.name: Sanrokuen
stops.note: This restaurant is inside a 150 year old traditional Japanese house and the atmosphere is incredible. You sit on the floor around a charcoal hearth called an irori and grill your own skewers of fish and meat.

stops.name: Saiko Iyashi-no-Sato Nemba (Traditional Village)
stops.note: Thatched-roof village with Fuji in the backdrop. Café on site if you haven't eaten

stops.name: Saiko Wild Bird Forest Park
stops.note: ¥100 sunflower seeds, birds on your hand, forest trail. ⚠️ Closed Thursdays.

stops.name: Narusawa Ice Cave
stops.note: The Narusawa Ice Cave is a lava tube located in the Aokigahara forest, in the part that belongs to Narusawa Village, Yamanashi Prefecture, Japan. It is one of the three larger lava tubes at the northern foot of Mount Fuji, the other two caves being Fugaku Wind Cave and Lake Sai Bat Cave. Short 15-20min walk through the cave.

options.name: Japanese Dining Suzukiya
options.note: A5 wagyu from the owner's father's farm, beautifully pressed sushi, unagi. The splurge dinner of the trip. Book well in advance. Dinner 5:30–9pm. ⚠️ Closed Tuesdays.

options.name: 炭焼ところ SUMIYAKI TOCORO.
options.note: Charcoal yakitori near your hotel. ⚠️ Opens 5pm, closes 7pm sharp.

stops.name: CAFETINO MT.FUJI
stops.note: Opens at 8.30am. Serves pancakes, sandwiches and pastas.

stops.name: Lake Motosu
stops.note: Take out a 1000 yen note, and you’ll see Lake Motosu on the back of it! Out of the five lakes, Lake Motosu has the clearest waters — making it a popular diving spot.

stops.name: Shiraito Falls
stops.note: A wide curtain of waterfalls fed directly by Mt. Fuji's snowmelt. On a clear day you can frame both the falls and Fuji in the same shot. Easy 1km loop walk, free entry, ¥500 parking.

stops.name: Makaino Farm Resort (Optional)
stops.note: Interactive farm with livestock, activities, play equipment, views of Mount Fuji & a restaurant.

stops.name: 金山テラス (Kanayama Terrace)
stops.note: 15 min from the falls. Floor-to-ceiling glass panels with a dramatic unobstructed Fuji view. Order the pork-deer burger. Note the ¥500/person parking surcharge applies even when dining. Open 10am–5pm.

stops.name: Fuji Shibazakura Festival
stops.note:
From mid-April to late May every year, the nearby Fuji Motosuko Resort plays host to the stunning Fuji Shibazakura Festival. Witness sprawling fields of vibrant pink moss, all against the magnificent backdrop of Mt. Fuji. Tour buses flood in after 11am. ¥1,000–1,300 entry + ¥500 parking. Walk the pink fields, soak your feet in the panoramic foot bath, grab food from the festival stalls. No need to book beforehand.
One of the best photo spots in from the Observation Deck at the back of the park. The raised platform allows you to get the best perspective of the rolling carpets of flowers with Mt Fuji in the background.

stops.name: XX
stops.note: XX

days.label: Day 8: 29 Apr (Wed)
days.title: A Gentle Last Full Day
days.desc: Back in Tokyo for one last full day. Keep it easy Driving Route

options.name: Haostay Coffee
options.note: Opens 7am. The earliest option by far. Right near the station, cosy interiors, large windows, great coffee, waffles and peanut butter toast. Charging spots and wifi too. Perfect if you want to be out the door by 7:30am.

options.name: enico.cafe & bar
options.note: Opens 7am. Same neighbourhood as Haostay. The eggs benedict with onsen tamago-style eggs is a standout, excellent espresso (La Marzocco machine), minimalist cabin vibe. One of the best-reviewed breakfast spots in the area.

stops.name: Hirano Beach, Lake Yamanakako
stops.note: 45min drive. Morning is the best time here — clearest skies, calmest water, best chance of a Fuji reflection. Swans on the lake, wide open shoreline, free parking. It's peaceful and unhurried. Don't linger past 10am to keep the day on schedule.

stops.name: Japanese Dining Suzukiya
stops.note: The perfect send-off meal. Suzukiya does lunch Wed–Sun, 11:30am–1:30pm. A5 wagyu set, pressed sushi, unagi — the same quality as dinner at a fraction of the price point. Book in advance, it fills up. ⚠️ Closed Tuesdays, lunch only available Wed–Sun.

stops.name: Top up petrol for rental car
stops.note: Save the receipt.

stops.name: Return rental car
stops.note: Return timing: 2pm

stops.name: Highway bus: Kawaguchiko → Shinjuku
stops.note:
Booked via highwaybus.com
Bus #1352 departs at 14:.40 from Kawaguchiko Station. Arrives at Shinjuku Busta at 16:25.

stops.name: Zauo Fishing Restaurant
stops.note:
Unique dinner right in the hotel — catch your own fish! Restaurant is right in the lobby of our hotel.
Reservation made for 5 pax at 7.30pm (Japan time).

stops.name: Komeda's Coffee Shinjuku First West
stops.note: Breakfast near the hotel

stops.name: Airport Limousine to Narita Airport
stops.note:
Check out from hotel at 11am. Board Airport Limousine. Journey is around 2 hours.
Board Airport Limousine Bus towards Narita International Terminal 1. Arrive at 14:10. Each passenger is allowed 2 luggages.

stops.name: Depart Narita for Singapore
stops.note:
Zipair ZG053
Departs at 16:50, arrives in Singapore at 23:00
NRT Terminal 1 - SIN Terminal 1
2 x LieFlat, 3 x Economy
